revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:7 nguŏk 15 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[hak:7-ngie̍t 15-ngit (nùng-la̍k)]][[ja:7月15日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 7월 15일]][[zh-min-nan:7 goe̍h 15 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:七月十五]][[zh:七月十五]]
{{Not to be confused with|July 15|qq=July 15}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=七月十五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 7월 15일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar July|qq=}} '''July 15''' is the 15th day of month {{ill|July (Lunisolar)|ja|7月 (旧暦)|lt=July (Fumizuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July (Lunisolar)|qq=}} the 7th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}} . == Events == * {{ill|Tenmu Emperor|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Emperor|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} 15th Year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|686|en|686|qid=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}} {{ill|August 9|en|August 9|qid=Q2782|lt=August 9|1=August 9}} ) – Governance was entrusted to the Empress and Crown Prince Kusakabe. * {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi|1=Engi (era)}} 1st Year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|901|en|901|qid=Q23580|lt=901|1=901}} {{ill|August 31|en|August 31|qid=Q2830|lt=August 31|1=August 31}} ) – In response to the {{ill|Xinyou Revolution|ja|Xinyou Revolution|lt=Xinyou Revolution|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xinyou Revolution|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , the era was changed from {{ill|Shōtai|en|Shōtai|qid=Q1324780|lt=Shōtai|1=Shōtai}} to {{ill|Engi (era)|en|Engi (era)|qid=Q1374325|lt=Engi|1=Engi (era)}} . * {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 6th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1721|en|1721|qid=Q6913|lt=1721|1=1721}} {{ill|August 7|en|August 7|qid=Q2781|lt=August 7|1=August 7}} ) – The puppet jōruri play “{{ill|The Woman-Killer and the Hell of Oil|en|The Woman-Killer and the Hell of Oil|qid=Q107404975|lt=Woman-Slaying, Oil Hell|1=The Woman-Killer and the Hell of Oil}} ” by {{ill|Chikamatsu Monzaemon|en|Chikamatsu Monzaemon|qid=Q324986|lt=Chikamatsu Monzaemon|1=Chikamatsu Monzaemon}} was first performed at Takemoto-za in Osaka.
== Births == * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1871|en|1871|qid=Q7746|lt=1871|1=1871}} {{ill|August 30|en|August 30|qid=Q2818|lt=August 30|1=August 30}} ) – {{ill|Doppo Kunikida|en|Doppo Kunikida|qid=Q374817|lt=Kunikida Doppo|1=Doppo Kunikida}} , novelist and poet (died {{ill|1908|en|1908|qid=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} ).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 3rd Year (leap year) ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|719|en|719|qid=Q23537|lt=719|1=719}} {{ill|September 3|en|September 3|qid=Q2860|lt=September 3|1=September 3}} ) – A member of the {{ill|Kibube|ja|忌部子人|lt=Kibube|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibube|1=Kibube|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11490720}} who was a central figure in the early compilation of the Imperial Annals and ancient records (birth year unknown). * {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|qid=Q1075605|lt=Shōwa (Japan)|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 9th Year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|842|en|842|qid=Q24730|lt=842|1=842}} {{ill|August 24|en|August 24|qid=Q2825|lt=August 24|1=August 24}} ) – {{ill|Emperor Saga|ja|Emperor Saga|lt=Emperor Saga|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Saga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} , the 52nd Emperor (born {{ill|786|en|786|qid=Q30307|lt=786|1=786}} ). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 4th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1595|en|1595|qid=Q6782|lt=1595|1=1595}} {{ill|August 20|en|August 20|qid=Q2824|lt=August 20|1=August 20}} ) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|en|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|qid=Q967501|lt=Toyotomi Hidetsugu|1=Toyotomi Hidetsugu}} , the adopted nephew of Regent {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} , (born {{ill|1568|en|1568|qid=Q6648|lt=1568|1=1568}} ). * {{ill|Genroku|1=Genroku|2=ja|3=元禄|qid=Q1325001|ja|Genroku|1=Genroku|2=ja|3=元禄|qid=Q1325001|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1325001}} 8th Year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1695|en|1695|qid=Q7696|lt=1695|1=1695}} {{ill|August 24|en|August 24|qid=Q2825|lt=August 24|1=August 24}} ) – {{ill|Enkū|en|Enkū|qid=Q2406940|lt=Enkū|1=Enkū}} , an itinerant monk (born {{ill|1632|en|1632|qid=Q6865|lt=1632|1=1632}} ).
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Ghost Festival|en|Ghost Festival|qid=Q696781|lt=Chūgen (Japan)|1=Ghost Festival}} : The custom of the “Three Gens” – designating January 15 as Jōgen, July 15 as Chūgen, and October 15 as Kagen – was transmitted from China. : In Japan, because it coincides with Obon, a custom developed to honor ancestral spirits and to present food offerings to one’s parents. : It is also a day for giving gifts to superiors and benefactors as an expression of gratitude. * {{ill|Obon|en|Obon|qid=Q1154888|lt=Obon|1=Obon}} / {{ill|Urabon-e|ja|盂蘭盆会|lt=Urabon-e|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urabon-e|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15898039}} (Japan) : A festival for honoring the spirits of ancestors. : Cucumbers and eggplants are fashioned into shapes resembling oxen or horses and offered.
== Comparison Table == The following shows the corresponding dates with the Gregorian calendar (New Calendar).
* {{ill|2017|en|2017|qid=Q25290|lt=Heisei 29|1=2017}} (2017) – September 05 * {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=Heisei 30|1=2018}} (2018) – August 25 * {{ill|2019|en|2019|qid=Q25274|lt=Reiwa 1|1=2019}} (2019) – August 15 * {{ill|2020|en|2020|qid=Q25337|lt=Reiwa 2|1=2020}} (2020) – September 02 * {{ill|2021|en|2021|qid=Q49628|lt=Reiwa 3|1=2021}} (2021) – August 22 * {{ill|2022|en|2022|qid=Q49625|lt=Reiwa 4|1=2022}} (2022) – August 12 * {{ill|2023|en|2023|qid=Q49622|lt=Reiwa 5|1=2023}} (2023) – August 30 * {{ill|2024|en|2024|qid=Q49619|lt=Reiwa 6|1=2024}} (2024) – August 18 * {{ill|2025|en|2025|qid=Q49616|lt=Reiwa 7|1=2025}} (2025) – September 06
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|7|14|7|16|6|15|8|7|0715|qq=}}
